item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-019","score":1,"pass_criteria_met":false,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer correctly wakes up to the SELA issue: it accepts that the master worker may currently tune more accurately, reframes hand tuning as benchmark/constraint rather than the long-term system, and identifies dependence on tacit expert knowledge, shift/material/equipment/product variation, and the need to sediment experience into rules/data/model feedback. It does not merely praise craftsmanship. However, it misses the required explicit comparison of order-of-magnitude differences in per-unit cost, throughput, and scalability, so it only partially satisfies the pass criteria."}
